executive_verdict.primary_failure_mode: The main architecture risk this run was mixed-era marketing certification evidence; that path is now fail-closed and freshly re-verified.
executive_verdict.most_urgent_fix: Keep the marketing coherence gate and registry-coverage guardrails in place as new loop surfaces and support jobs evolve.

audit_metadata.inspection_note: Direct cron tool inspection was restricted in this watchdog run, so the live Gateway jobs file plus current runtime artifacts and code were used as the mutable source of truth.
relevant_live_jobs_checked: Push research findings to git repo

owns: bounded timeout reruns and timeout tuning
syncing research artifacts into the git repo

findings.title: Marketing certification previously allowed mixed-era artifacts to support a pass decision
findings.mechanism: The checker and independent verifier could accept a healthy current momentum artifact while still trusting an older runner bundle, which weakened evidence coherence across the marketing full-contract loop.
findings.root_cause: Artifact freshness was checked, but cross-artifact temporal coherence was not enforced.
findings.recommended_fix: Require the runner artifact to be coherent with newer peer artifacts before checker or independent verifier can issue a pass, then regenerate the runner bundle before certification.

findings.title: Architecture ownership boundaries are explicit and currently non-overlapping
findings.mechanism: The runtime registry covers live architecture-scoped Gateway jobs, full-contract loops have current pass artifacts, and owner-only support loops remain explicitly out of self-certifying roles.
findings.root_cause: Previous registry drift repairs remain effective and are now corroborated by fresh loop-integrity and health-monitor outputs.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep self_improvement_loops.json aligned with live Gateway jobs and preserve the fail-closed registry coverage invariant.

findings.title: Learning promotion is working: repeated audit findings are landing as runtime gates instead of report-only prose
findings.mechanism: The marketing coherence issue was promoted directly into checker and independent-verifier code, then revalidated with fresh runner and loop-integrity outputs.
findings.root_cause: The runtime now converts discovered certification drift into executable guardrails instead of relying on future operator memory.
findings.recommended_fix: Continue promoting repeated architecture findings into typed checks and fail-closed gates, especially when multiple artifacts can drift out of sync.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Preserve cross-artifact coherence gates on full-contract loops
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: They prevent stale runner evidence from silently certifying a healthy-looking but mixed-era loop state.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Independent verification remains evidence-coherent instead of passable from stitched-together artifacts.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Keep the loop registry synchronized with active Gateway jobs
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: The topology is healthy now because the registry remains the authoritative ownership map.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: No hidden live loop can escape architecture accounting or create overlap silently.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Keep converting repeated audit discoveries into code-level gates
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: That is the clearest proof that the runtime is actually learning instead of only rephrasing earlier findings.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Better loop resilience, less self-certification drift, and faster recovery from future architecture regressions.

repairs_applied_this_run.change: Added a fail-closed runner-to-peer coherence gate so the checker refuses mixed-era runner, audit, and momentum evidence.
Added the same coherence guard to independent verification so a pass cannot be issued from mixed-era artifacts.
Regenerated the full marketing runner bundle after the coherence-gate repair so the live certification path is based on fresh aligned artifacts.

independent_verification.summary: Fresh post-repair verification confirms the new marketing coherence guard, a fresh passing marketing independent-verification artifact, green loop-integrity output, and a zero-issue health monitor result.

highest_risk_unresolved_issue.title: Owner-only loops still rely on owner-local proof surfaces rather than a single normalized registry-wide verifier contract
highest_risk_unresolved_issue.why: That is acceptable by design today, but it remains the loosest part of the architecture compared with the full-contract loops.
